risks: No network isolation — all resources use public network access (Key Vault, Storage, Cosmos DB, ML Workspace), increasing attack surface
Single-region deployment (eastus) with no geo-redundancy or availability zones; Cosmos DB isZoneRedundant is false
Storage account and Cosmos DB local auth (key access) not explicitly disabled — best practice recommends managed identity-only access
No resource tagging strategy defined for cost tracking, governance, or environment identification
Cosmos DB provisioned throughput requires upfront RU/s sizing — risk of under- or over-provisioning without autoscale configured at the container level
correctionsRecommended: Enable private endpoints for Key Vault, Storage Account, Cosmos DB, and ML Workspace to restrict public network exposure
Enable availability zones on Cosmos DB (set isZoneRedundant to true) for production-grade single-region resilience
Explicitly disable storage account key access (set allowSharedKeyAccess to false) and disable Cosmos DB key-based auth to enforce managed identity usage
Add resource tags (e.g., environment, project, costCenter) to all resources for governance and cost management
Configure Cosmos DB autoscale throughput at the database or container level to handle traffic spikes without manual RU/s sizing
Consider adding a secondary Cosmos DB region for disaster recovery if the workload grows to require multi-region availability
